statement: For any candidate model M, the paper-defined (unnormalized) expected MIRA score is at least 1/2.

domain.candidate: conditional distribution whose radial CDF about a fixed center is continuous, so lambda_n is Uniform[0,1]
domain.truth: conditional distribution measured on the same nested metric balls
domain.coupling: lambda_k = T(lambda_n) almost surely for a measurable nondecreasing T:[0,1]->[0,1]

machine_checkable_contract.required_inequality: 2 E[U T(U)] - E[T(U)] >= 0 for U~Uniform[0,1] and nondecreasing T

normalization_note: The released implementation divides by (N+1)/(N+2); its corresponding floor is (N+2)/(2(N+1)), which is strictly above 1/2.
falsification_condition: A valid nondecreasing T under the stated radial-CDF assumptions with exact score below 1/2.
blocked_condition: The source assumptions or score definition cannot be resolved unambiguously.
